tasks.description: Turn the completed research matrix into one decision note that names the primary bridge path, the fallback path, and the reasons for deferring the remaining transport options for now. This is now shipped through the merged research detail and hub docs on branch `docs/native-planning-runtime-terminal-bridge-feasibility`, commit `03ef824`, and PR `#359`.
tasks.priority_reason: The path decision is already documented and merged, so queue follow-up should move to the blocked readiness gate for any local attach spike rather than keep this as a pending proposal.

tasks.direction_relation_note: This task closes the implementation-direction entry gate by recording the tmux local-attach evidence and verdict in docs/plan/27 and docs/plan/28 before the first real adapter slice starts.
tasks.title: Gate tmux local-attach spike on capability audit and evidence
tasks.description: Track the preconditions for any tmux-based local attach spike after the completed bridge research pass. This gate is now closed by the executed local evidence in docs/plan/27-terminal-agent-tmux-local-attach-readiness-evidence.md and the verdict in docs/plan/28-terminal-agent-tmux-local-attach-gate-verdict.md, which record attach discovery, prompt injection, incremental relay, interrupt truth, approval truth, recovery anchors, and operator failure signatures without adding a provider adapter yet.
tasks.priority_reason: The readiness evidence and verdict now exist as reviewable docs plus captured artifacts, so this prerequisite should stop blocking queue progression and hand off directly to the first real tmux implementation slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: With docs/plan/27 and docs/plan/28 closing the tmux readiness gate, the next executable slice is the first reviewable tmux adapter entrypoint rather than a broad all-at-once bridge rollout.
tasks.title: Land the first tmux local-attach adapter entrypoint
tasks.description: Implement the first reviewable tmux-only bridge slice by wiring a concrete tmux adapter through `StartupProbe` and `TerminalBridgeAttachment`, validating `tmux` presence plus target pane or session-handle resolution, surfacing missing-server and missing-target copy before shell entry, and exposing `LocalAttach + SessionHandle` with truthful `ManualHandoff` approval and `RuntimeNative` interrupt semantics. Leave live relay, transcript recovery, completion heuristics, and optional catalog discovery to follow-up slices.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest operator request still leaves this tmux attach and probe slice as the highest-priority executable item. Keep it as the single ready head so execution can resume from one concrete bridge implementation task while the remaining tmux follow-ups stay blocked behind it.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The gate verdict and latest main-session answer both call out live relay, transcript recovery, interrupt, and completion detection as the next bounded follow-up once the tmux entrypoint exists.
tasks.title: Add tmux live relay, recovery capture, and turn completion
tasks.description: Extend the tmux bridge after the attach entrypoint lands by implementing prompt submission via literal paste, incremental output relay via `pipe-pane`, transcript recovery via `capture-pane`, interrupt requests via `C-c`, and transcript or terminal-derived completion heuristics without inventing provider turn events or widening into wrapper, SSH, or proxy paths.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest execution-focused summary made this a concrete queued follow-up, not just a candidate. It remains blocked only because the active tmux entrypoint slice must land first so relay and completion work can build on a real attachment path.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The capability-boundary docs keep `SessionCatalog` optional, so any tmux discovery or reattach UX should follow the explicit handle path instead of leading it.
tasks.title: Add optional tmux session catalog and reattach surface
tasks.description: Once the explicit tmux pane or session-handle attach path is stable, add an optional tmux discovery surface that exposes handle-based reattach without making catalog availability mandatory. Keep attach-only flows valid, and do not turn tmux discovery into a fake provider session model.
tasks.priority_reason: This is now explicit queued follow-up work because the latest summary named the optional session-catalog surface as a concrete post-entrypoint slice. It stays blocked behind the active tmux entrypoint so attach-only and explicit-handle flows can stabilize first.
